name: sdlc-workflow
description: Complete SDLC workflow as six skill-mode routers plus a setup-wide-logging command. /wf executes the canonical 13-stage lifecycle (shape, slice, plan, implement, verify, review, handoff, ship, retro) plus four perf/observability augmentations (instrument, experiment, benchmark, profile). /wf-quick starts compressed flows (quick, rca, investigate, discover, hotfix, update-deps, refactor, ideate, simplify). /wf-meta navigates and manages existing workflows (next, status, resume, sync, amend, extend, skip, close, how, announce, init-ship-plan). /wf-design dispatches across 22 design sub-commands (shape, craft, audit, critique, plus 15 transformations and 3 setup/extract operators) in three invocation modes — workflow stage 2b (writes 02b-design.md), workflow + sub-command (writes 02c-craft.md, 07-design-audit.md, 07-design-critique.md, or design-notes/<sub>-<timestamp>.md depending on category), and freestanding (no workflow). /wf-docs runs the documentation pipeline (discover → audit → plan → generate → review) or invokes a single Diátaxis primitive — plan, tutorial, how-to, reference, explanation, readme, review — to write one document. /review runs per-dimension or named-sweep code review across 31 dimensions and 7 sweeps via parallel sub-agent dispatch. The wf-validate pre-write hook gates artifact writes against schema. Workflow-type aware: compressed mode (workflow-type: quick → reads 01-quick.md), forwarded mode (workflow-type: rca/investigate → reads 01-rca.md/01-investigate.md as rich context behind the synthesized 02-shape.md), standard mode (per-slice files). No-optional-artifacts policy enforced: every stage's Conditional Inputs row is mandatory when present. Per-slice review artifacts (07-review-<slice>.md + 07-review-<slice>-<command>.md) so reviewing a second slice never overwrites the first. wf-handoff refuses to proceed if any per-slice review has unresolved blockers. Augmentations register on 00-index.md and produce type-specific deliverables that downstream stages consume: instrument adds 04b-instrument.md signals; experiment writes 04c-experiment.md cohort/rollback design; benchmark writes 05c-benchmark.md baseline/compare; design-* writes design-notes/<sub>-<timestamp>.md and 02b-design.md / 02c-craft.md visual contracts. v9.5.0 overhauled handoff and ship: handoff stage 8 added a PR-readiness block (commitlint, public-surface drift, doc-mirror regen, PR comment triage with bounded 5-iteration loop, rebase onto base, live PR readiness check) producing readiness-verdict that ship gates on; ship stage 9 split into a project-level .ai/ship-plan.md (authored once via /wf-meta init-ship-plan with 6 templates: kotlin-maven-central, npm-public, pypi, container-image, server-deploy, library-internal) and per-release accumulating run artifacts 09-ship-run-<run-id>.md indexed by 09-ship-runs.md, replayable via 13 idempotent steps that resume from status: awaiting-input. v9.4.0 promoted /wf-docs to its own skill router and folded the 7 standalone Diátaxis skills (diataxis-doc-planner, tutorial-writer, how-to-guide-writer, reference-writer, explanation-writer, readme-writer, docs-reviewer) into it as primitive references. v9.3.0 folded /wf-design into the same skill-router shape as the other four routers (the standalone commands/wf-design.md was removed; the design skill now dispatches directly). v9.0.0-alpha.4 collapsed the 13 lifecycle slash commands into the /wf skill router; alpha.3 collapsed 10 lifecycle-navigation commands into /wf-meta; alpha.2 collapsed 10 standalone-workflow commands into /wf-quick; alpha.1 collapsed 38 review slash commands into the /review skill. Release notes for every v8.x feature live in CHANGELOG.md.
